section.name: port-allocation
section.summary: 100-port range convention for multi-project VPS port assignment, port-registry.txt as single source of truth, and per-project directory layout.

rule.statement: Allocate 100-port blocks per project (8100-8199, 8200-8299) and maintain /srv/port-registry.txt as the single source of truth for all active port assignments.
rule.rationale: Without a registry, port conflicts are discovered at service startup, often during a deploy. The registry makes the full picture visible in one place so a new project can pick a free range without scanning running processes.

rule.statement: Always bind application ports to 127.0.0.1, never 0.0.0.0; nginx is the only service that listens on public interfaces.
rule.rationale: Binding to 0.0.0.0 exposes application ports directly to the internet. A misconfigured firewall or missed ufw rule becomes a direct breach. Binding to 127.0.0.1 makes nginx the mandatory ingress point.

list.item: 8100-8199 — Project 1 (API at +00, SPA at +01, admin at +10, dev at +50)

example.code:
# /srv/port-registry.txt # Format: PORT SERVICE PROJECT BIND NOTES 5432 postgresql shared 127.0.0.1 Docker container 5672 rabbitmq-amqp shared 127.0.0.1 Message broker 6379 redis shared 127.0.0.1 Cache + Celery backend 15672 rabbitmq-mgmt shared 127.0.0.1 RabbitMQ admin UI
8100 nero-api nero 127.0.0.1 FastAPI API + WebSocket 8101 nero-web nero 127.0.0.1 React SPA
8200 meetingtax-be meetingtax 127.0.0.1 FastAPI API 8201 meetingtax-fe meetingtax 127.0.0.1 Next.js frontend

section.reference: Port allocation registry with format header and example entries for shared and project-specific ports
Script: create runtime dirs, .env, systemd service, update port registry for a new project
